mission.title: kcp: APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its schemas until the first APIBinding is created
mission.description: APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its schemas until the first APIBinding is created. This issue affects 1+ users.

steps.title: Identify kcp troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kcp deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kcp
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `controller-runtime.source       if kind is a CRD, it should be installed before calling Start   {"kind": "Widget.data.my`

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kcp resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Describe the bug**
A controller using APIExports can't successfully start up and wildcard list/watch the APIs it's exporting until the first APIBinding is created.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reprodu

steps.title: Apply the fix for APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its schemas unt
Confirm APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its s is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `controller-runtime.source       if kind is a CRD, it should be installed before calling Start   {"kind": "Widget.data.my` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: This issue was resolved by applying the fix from the linked PR. The root cause was identified and addressed by the community.
codeSnippets: 1.6541008964721239e+09  ERROR   controller-runtime.source       if kind is a CRD, it should be installed before calling Start   {"kind": "Widget.data.my.domain", "error": "no matches for kind \"Widget\" in version \"data.my.domain/v1alpha1\""}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kcp installed or the issue environment reproducible.
